content.text: Now I have the exact tab counts. I'll use a Python script to make both replacements precisely.

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
filepath = '/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/src/decorators/log-execution.decorator.ts'

with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Change 1: instance.warning(string, object) -> instance.warning({ err: string, context: object })
old1 = (
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tinstance.warning(\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t`[LogExecution]: Long execution ${executionTime.toFixed(2)}ms.`,\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{ ...successLogContext, success: true },\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t);"
)
new1 = (
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tinstance.warning({\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\terr: `[LogExecution]: Long execution ${executionTime.toFixed(2)}ms.`,\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontext: { ...successLogContext, success: true },\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t});"
)

if old1 in content:
    content = content.replace(old1, new1)
    print("Change 1 applied successfully.")
else:
    print("Change 1: pattern NOT found!")

# Change 2: instance.error(string, object) -> instance.error({ err: string, context: object })
old2 = (
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\tinstance.error(`Method ${className}.${methodName} failed.`, {\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t...errorLogContext,\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\terrorMessage: (error as Error).message,\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\terrorStack: (error as Error).stack,\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tsuccess: false,\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t});"
)
new2 = (
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\tinstance.error({\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\terr: `Method ${className}.${methodName} failed.`,\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontext: {\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t...errorLogContext,\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\terrorMessage: (error as Error).message,\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\terrorStack: (error as Error).stack,\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tsuccess: false,\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n"
    "\t\t\t\t\t\t\t});"
)

if old2 in content:
    content = content.replace(old2, new2)
    print("Change 2 applied successfully.")
else:
    print("Change 2: pattern NOT found!")

with open(filepath, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    f.write(content)

print("File written.")
EOF
